rendered_lines: Despite 2020 being unprecedented, I am more optimistic than ever about the prospects for the Asian life insurance industry and AIA's future. We continue to challenge
ourselves and evolve our priorities to remain responsive to changing customer expectations and financial needs. Our new corporate strategy builds on AIA's
existing strengths and advances our transformation to deliver the best possible service to customers.

rendered_lines: 2020 Annual Report 011

The primary impact of the pandemic on our 2020 financial performance was on new business sales, as containment measures in many of our markets restricted face-to-face sales activities, particularly in the
first half of 2020. Although VONB of US$2,765 million in 2020 was 33% lower than in 2019,
we rapidly adopted new digital tools and the general easing of movement restrictions supported a recovery in sales momentum. AIA China was the largest contributor to the Group's VONB in 2020 and was
our first reporting segment to show a recovery in sales momentum, as the region progressively eased strict lockdowns from March 2020.
Excluding Mainland Chinese visitor sales to Hong Kong, which remained at low levels, VONB across our other segments recovered strongly in the second half, growing 23% compared to the first half...

rendered_lines: Embedded value (EV) equity as at 31 December 2020 reached a record high of US$67.185 billion, up US$5.277 billion before dividends of US$1.997 billion,

rendered_by_block.p19_b9: Embedded value (EV) equity as at 31 December 2020 reached a record high of US$67.185 billion, up US$5.277 billion before dividends of US$1.997 billion,

rendered_lines: This was driven by EV operating profit of US$7,243 million, offsetting lower government bond yields. EV operating profit included
US$549 million from positive operating variances, reflecting continued positive overall experience relative to our EV assumptions.

rendered_lines: Our high-quality, recurring earnings and active in-force portfolio management drove 5% OPAT growth to US$5,942 million, maintaining a stable 16.9% operating margin.
Renewal premiums received rose 10%, with recurring premiums exceeding 90% of total 2020 premiums received. OPAT growth benefited from significantly positive
claims experience throughout the year,
offsetting lower yields on new fixed income investments and assumed reduction in long-term equity investment returns. As expected,
the exceptionally positive medical claims experience in the first half did not recur in the second half, but
continued contributing positively to OPAT growth. Persistency improved in the second half as lapse experience in AIA Thailand normalised,
with Group persistency at 95%.

rendered_by_block.p19_b12: Our high-quality, recurring earnings and active in-force portfolio management drove 5% OPAT growth to US$5,942 million, maintaining a stable 16.9% operating margin.

lower expected investment returns. The Group's financial position remained very strong as at 31 December 2020, with Free Surplus of
US$13.473 billion after dividends of US$1.997 billion.

rendered_lines: As at 31 December 2020, the solvency ratio of our principal operating company, AIA Company Limited (AIA Co.), remained very strong at 489%.
As at 31 December 2020, under the new group supervisory framework, the Group LCSM cover ratio was 374%. We will disclose the Group LCSM
cover ratio as the primary measure of the Group's regulatory solvency.

rendered_lines: The Board has proposed a final dividend of 100.30 Hong Kong cents per share, an increase of 7.5%, subject to shareholders' approval at the Company's forthcoming Annual
General Meeting. This brings the total dividend for 2020 to 135.30 Hong Kong cents per share,
an increase of 6.9% over the total dividend for 2019. This is in line with AIA's long-standing prudent, sustainable and progressive dividend policy, which
allows the Group to capture future growth opportunities while maintaining financial flexibility.

rendered_lines: Although VONB of US$2,765 million in 2020 was 33% lower than in 2019, we rapidly adopted new digital tools and sales momentum was supported by widespread
easing of movement restrictions.
AIA China was the largest contributor to Group VONB in 2020 and our first reporting segment to show recovery in sales momentum as the
region progressively eased strict lockdowns from March 2020. Excluding Mainland Chinese visitor sales in Hong Kong, which remained low,
VONB in the rest of the Group recovered strongly in the second half, up 23% versus the first half. Group new business momentum has continued to be strong in 2021,
with VONB in the first two months 15% higher than in the same period in 2020.

rendered_by_block.p20_b21:
Although VONB of US$2,765 million in 2020 was 33% lower than in 2019, we rapidly adopted new digital tools and sales momentum was supported by widespread
easing of movement restrictions.
rendered_by_block.p20_b22: AIA China was the largest contributor to Group VONB in 2020 and our first reporting segment to show recovery in sales momentum as the
rendered_by_block.p20_b23: region progressively eased strict lockdowns from March 2020. Excluding Mainland Chinese visitor sales in Hong Kong, which remained low,
rendered_by_block.p20_b24: VONB in the rest of the Group recovered strongly in the second half, up 23% versus the first half. Group new business momentum has continued to be strong in 2021,
rendered_by_block.p20_b25: with VONB in the first two months 15% higher than in the same period in 2020.

rendered_lines: AIA China entered 2021 with strong momentum, with VONB in the first two months recording very strong growth versus the same period in 2020.
VONB of US$968 million in 2020 was 17% lower than 2019, primarily due to limited sales during the initial COVID-19 outbreak in the first quarter. New
business momentum improved rapidly as movement restrictions eased. VONB resumed our customary seasonal pattern, weighted more towards the first half.
In 2020, AIA China was the largest contributor to Group VONB.

rendered_by_block.p20_b26: AIA China entered 2021 with strong momentum, with VONB in the first two months recording very strong growth versus the same period in 2020.

rendered_by_block.p20_b29: In 2020, AIA China was the largest contributor to Group VONB.

rendered_lines: Following travel restrictions, new business sales from Mainland Chinese visitors to AIA Hong Kong have been effectively suspended since early February 2020, resulting
in a significant decline in VONB.
The VONB margin decreased, reflecting underwriting expense overruns due to reduced new business volumes, product mix changes and lower interest rates.
Macau resumed the Individual Visit Scheme for Mainland China travelers at end-September 2020, supporting recovery in sales to Mainland Chinese visitors in Macau,
which accounted for over one-third of AIA Macau's total ANP in the fourth quarter.
